notes: odoo-wave is the INTERNAL git-executor: user-invocable=false, consume-only, dispatched by run-harness per coding wave-layer with an APPROVED plan. Evals cover: (1) NOT a user front door - a user prompt routes through odoo-intake -> odoo-planning, not directly into odoo-wave; (2) consume-only - no plan-gate, no self-derive of module-DAG/topology; (3) safety verify - the disjoint file-ownership audit still runs (trust-but-verify) and catches a bad plan; (4) B1 dispatch topology - INVOKES odoo-coding per WI from the orchestrating context and never chooses agent/model (Decision X); (5) saga rollback on mid-wave failure; (6) gate split (B2) - opens PR + squash + STOPS at the L2-squash-gate and NEVER merges (merge owned by odoo-pr-monitoring); (7) squash tree-identity abort; (8) conflict-resolver path. Each case has a precise expected_behavior so it fails for the right reason (ETHOS#11).

evals.prompt: Do this as a wave: parallelize these 3 changes and land them without touching main.
evals.expected_behavior: odoo-wave is user-invocable=false and does NOT fire from a user prompt. The request routes to odoo-intake -> odoo-planning (which produces the plan); run-harness later dispatches odoo-wave per coding wave-layer.
evals.must_not: Trigger odoo-wave directly from the user prompt, or create any branch/worktree before an approved plan exists.

evals.prompt: Context: run-harness dispatches odoo-wave for a coding wave-layer with the plan's WI list, wave-batched module-DAG, and `independent` topology already provided.
evals.expected_behavior: odoo-wave CONSUMES the plan's module-DAG + topology verbatim - it does NOT recompute them, does NOT emit a plan gate, and does NOT scale by WI count. Phase 0 runs the safety audit, then Phase 1 creates the integration branch + worktrees.
evals.must_not: Re-derive the module-DAG/topology, emit a human plan-gate, or run a standalone fallback path.

evals.prompt: Context: the dispatched plan maps models.py to both WI-A and WI-B (file-ownership overlap).
evals.expected_behavior: Phase 0.2 disjoint file-ownership audit runs even though the plan supplied the map (trust-but-verify). It finds models.py in both scopes -> STOP BLOCKED, report the overlap, route back to odoo-planning to re-partition. No worktree is created.
evals.must_not: Skip the ownership audit because the plan provided the map, or create worktrees with an overlapping file map.

evals.prompt: Context: odoo-wave is executing a 3-WI wave-layer; each WI must be implemented.
evals.expected_behavior: Per WI, odoo-wave INVOKES the odoo-coding skill via the Skill tool from its orchestrating context (B1), passing the WI's worktree path + module set + DAG slice + design pointer. odoo-coding owns the coder count + model; odoo-wave never resolves a model tier itself (Decision X). odoo-coding returns the commit SHA(s); odoo-wave cherry-picks.
evals.must_not: Dispatch anonymous WI workers with a 'you ARE the specialist' brief, choose a model tier per WI, or set a coder count.

evals.prompt: Context: the plan's module-DAG declares WI-B depends_on WI-A.
evals.expected_behavior: odoo-wave cherry-picks A onto integration first, then lazily forks WI-B's worktree from the updated integration, invokes odoo-coding for B, and cherry-picks B. The depends_on edge is consumed from the plan, never recomputed.
evals.must_not: Recompute the dependency edge, or fork WI-B's worktree before WI-A is cherry-picked onto integration.

evals.prompt: Context: a cherry-pick's post-pick verify cannot be made green within the loop's bound.
evals.expected_behavior: odoo-wave applies the integration-loop.md saga: either clean-abort (reset-hard to the pre-wave SHA) or resume from the last passing checkpoint, never leaving a half-built integration branch. Reports the failing WI.
evals.must_not: Leave a half-built integration branch (an applied-but-unverified cherry-pick or conflict markers in the tree), or continue past an unrecoverable failure.

evals.prompt: Context: all WIs are cherry-picked, reviewed, and the integration branch is green.
evals.expected_behavior: odoo-wave opens 1 PR, squashes with tree-identity verification, and STOPS at the L2-squash-gate. It does NOT merge. The merge is owned by odoo-pr-monitoring at the L2-merge-gate; odoo-wave records the PR URL + squashed SHA + tree-identity result and emits next: odoo-pr-monitoring.
evals.must_not: Merge the PR, auto-squash-and-merge, or perform post-merge cleanup (those belong to odoo-pr-monitoring).

evals.prompt: Context: squash step on a 3-WI integration branch. git diff --quiet wave-backup-<slug> exits with code 1.
evals.expected_behavior: Squash tree-identity FAILED. odoo-wave aborts: reports the mismatch, restores from the backup tag, and does NOT force-push. Reports which files differ.
evals.must_not: Force-push when the tree-identity check fails, or silently continue after a failed squash verification.

evals.prompt: Context: WI-A and WI-B both unexpectedly modified __init__.py (missed by the plan). Cherry-pick of WI-B fails with a merge conflict.
evals.expected_behavior: Phase 3: odoo-wave dispatches a brief Sonnet resolver subagent (worker-brief.md) with the conflicting diff and both WI briefs. The resolver edits the files (markers removed) and returns; odoo-wave invokes the git-toolkit:git-ops skill to run cherry-pick --continue, then re-runs verify and checkpoints.
evals.must_not: Let the resolver run any git op (stage/commit/cherry-pick continue), silently skip the conflict, or skip re-running verify after resolution.
